design_philosophy: KinnectCare is a family safety and senior wellness mobile app. The design avoids the cold, clinical feel of traditional healthcare apps and the harsh tech-heavy look of tracking apps. Instead, it uses an 'Organic & Earthy' archetype to establish warmth, trust, and calm. Following E1's rules, it prioritizes extremely high contrast and legibility for users aged 35-75. The design relies on tactile, generous spacing (p-6 to p-8), rounded edges, skeuomorphic familiarity (where helpful), and clear typographic hierarchy.

buttons.primary: Solid #1B5E35 background, White text, 56px height, 16px border-radius. Active state scales down 0.98. Used for major actions (Get Started, Confirm).
buttons.sos_emergency: Circular floating button or massive bottom CTA. Solid #DC2626, White text, pulse animation or heavy shadow to stand out. Height 64px.

cards.base: White (#FFFFFF) background, 24px border-radius, soft green-tinted shadow. Content inside must have 20px padding.

inputs.text: Background #F9F5F0, 12px border-radius, 16px padding, 16px font-size. Border 1px solid #E3EDE0. Focused state: 2px solid #2D8C55.

accessibility_rules.contrast: Ensure all text meets minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio. Primary text #062210 against #F9F5F0 or #FFFFFF easily passes.
accessibility_rules.touch_targets: Minimum touch target size is 48x48. Buttons are 56px tall.
accessibility_rules.icons: Use lucide-react-native. Icons should be recognizable (e.g., Pill for medication, Bell for alerts, Shield for safety).
accessibility_rules.test_ids: All interactive elements must include 'testID' (React Native equivalent of data-testid). Use kebab-case.

splash_bg.description: Abstract fluid green/white background for splash screen. Overlay with #F9F5F0 at 80% opacity for text legibility.

splash.layout: Full screen centered content. Logo at top, big bold welcome text. Primary 'Get Started' CTA at bottom.
splash.visuals: Use splash_bg as ImageBackground with #F9F5F0/80 overlay.

dashboard.layout: Header with 'Hello, [Name]' and settings icon. ScrollView. 'Your Family' horizontal or vertical list of member cards. 'Reminders' section. Big SOS button anchored at bottom or bottom tab bar.
dashboard.cards: Member card includes Avatar, Name, Age, Status Dot, and 'Last seen' text.

alerts.layout: List view of alerts. Differentiate alerts by background tint (e.g., missed check-in in Red tint, low battery in Amber tint).
alerts.interaction: Swipe to dismiss or explicit 'Acknowledge' button.

check_in.layout: Success screen. Massive green checkmark in center (use tertiary green background for circle). 'You are checked in!' text.
check_in.visuals: Animated checkmark if possible. Large 'Done' button.

add_member.layout: Form layout in ScrollView. Generous spacing between inputs.
add_member.inputs: Name, Age, Phone, Gender. Submit button at bottom.

member_detail.layout: Header with Avatar and Name. Map preview snippet (or mock icon) for 'Location'. 'Last seen' timestamp. List of 'Care Reminders'.

instructions_to_main_agent: Build the app using React Native/Expo. Since there are no web CSS globals, use StyleSheet or NativeWind/Tailwind depending on setup.
Implement the colors exactly as specified. Do not introduce grays for text; use the dark green tints (text_primary, text_secondary) to keep the app feeling organic.
Ensure all text elements are readable (no tiny text). Use the provided font scale.
For the SOS button, make it highly prominent—it is a critical safety feature.
Mock the GPS map with a placeholder image or a simple view if react-native-maps is not configured.
All interactive elements MUST have a testID prop (e.g. testID='login-button') for testing.
Use the provided image URLs via the Image component with a uri source.
